steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 concludes that The Nutcracker should no longer be produced because it is outdated. The question asks how Text 2 responds to that view.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Text 2 agrees the show is outdated and should be discontinued': Text 2 explicitly argues the show should be kept, not discontinued. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Text 2 disputes that the show is old-fashioned': Text 2 actually concedes the show is outdated — it disagrees only with the conclusion to discontinue it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Text 2 argues the show generates important income': Revenue is a supporting detail in Text 2, not its central counterargument. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Text 2 acknowledges the show is dated but argues it should be kept and modernized': Text 2 grants the show is outdated yet directly opposes Text 1's conclusion by arguing it should be kept — pointing to Debbie Allen's Hot Chocolate Nutcracker as proof that creative modernization works. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
